entities.name: TopoLVM Capacity-Aware Storage
entities.description: Capacity-aware Kubernetes PVC storage provisioner using physical volume groups on host nodes. Default volume group vg-general in Genestack. Listed in Genestack architecture Storage Systems layer alongside Longhorn, Rook Ceph, iSCSI, RBD, and NFS. rackspacecloud.com/genestack-structure-and-files.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Data stored on host node LVM volume groups (vg-general) — encryption dependent on host-level LUKS or dm-crypt configuration which is unconfirmed
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: N/A — local node storage provisioner; data does not traverse network for TopoLVM volumes (node-local storage)
encryption_profile.key_management: Host-level encryption keys if LUKS enabled; no centralized key management for TopoLVM itself

availability_design.redundancy_level: Runs as DaemonSet on all storage-capable nodes; capacity-aware scheduling prevents over-provisioning. No cross-node replication — node-local storage only

observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: Volume group utilization, PVC provisioning success/failure, node capacity
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Volume group capacity thresholds, provisioning failures

support_model.escalation_path: OpenStack Genestack Engineering → Storage/Infrastructure

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Kubernetes PVC provisioning on node-local storage fails. Pods requiring local persistent volumes cannot be scheduled. Genestack services depending on TopoLVM-backed PVCs (MariaDB, RabbitMQ, other stateful services) affected if rescheduled.

entities.access_control_model: Kubernetes RBAC — TopoLVM CSI driver operates with cluster-level privileges; PVC access controlled by Kubernetes namespace RBAC; no end-user direct access to LVM volume groups

temporal.change_reason: Attribute enrichment — Genestack node-local storage provisioner attributes populated

provenance.primary_data_source: docs

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Host-level LUKS/dm-crypt encryption for TopoLVM volume groups unconfirmed — node-local storage may contain unencrypted OpenStack service data (MariaDB databases, RabbitMQ persistent queues)
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Audit LUKS encryption status on all Genestack node LVM volume groups (vg-general); enforce encryption for volumes backing stateful services

known_data_gaps.gap_description: No cross-node replication for TopoLVM volumes — node failure results in data loss for any PVCs on that node. Single point of failure for node-local storage
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Evaluate migration strategy for critical stateful services from TopoLVM to replicated storage (Ceph/Longhorn) for services requiring HA

Docker is primarily used for image building (Dockerfiles), local development, and CI/CD build stages. Production impact is indirect through broken build pipelines.

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred — container runtime with root-level access on hosts; container escape vulnerabilities are recurring CVE category

known_data_gaps.gap_description: K8s clusters use containerd as CRI (sys-085). Docker is listed as 'critical' but its actual production role may be limited to image builds and dev environments. Criticality may be overstated.
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Clarify Docker's role — if used only for builds, downgrade criticality to 'high'; if used as production CRI on non-K8s hosts, maintain 'critical'

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) grants root-equivalent access. Unknown if socket is exposed to containers (common misconfiguration) or restricted
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Audit Docker socket permissions; ensure no containers mount docker.sock; implement rootless Docker where possible

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Unknown if Docker Content Trust (DCT / DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1) is enforced for image pulls
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Enable DCT on build servers and CI/CD runners

entities.name: cert-manager TLS Certificate Management
entities.description: Kubernetes-native certificate management controller for Genestack. Release notes reference cert-manager's ExperimentalGatewayAPISupport option. Automates TLS certificate issuance and renewal for OpenStack service endpoints exposed via Envoy Gateway API. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: TLS private keys stored as K8s Secrets (etcd encryption dependency)
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: cert-manager controller communicates with ACME/CA endpoints over TLS; webhook serves over TLS
encryption_profile.key_management: Automated certificate lifecycle management; private keys in K8s Secrets; CA issuer configuration per ClusterIssuer/Issuer CRD

availability_design.redundancy_level: Single active controller with leader election; K8s restart policy

observability_stack.monitoring_tool: Prometheus (cert-manager native metrics including certificate expiry)

support_model.escalation_path: SRE → Platform Engineering → Jetstack/cert-manager Community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: TLS certificate renewal halts across all Genestack services; existing certificates continue working until expiry; after expiry, cascading TLS failures across ALL OpenStack API endpoints exposed via Envoy (sys-182); SAML federation broken
affected_capabilities: Certificate renewal for all OpenStack endpoints

entities.access_control_model: Kubernetes RBAC - cert-manager controller service account with cluster-wide Secret read/write for certificate storage; ClusterIssuer/Issuer CRDs for policy

backup_status.retention_period: N/A - certificates and keys stored in K8s Secrets (etcd backup covers these)

temporal.change_reason: KG enrichment - attribute population from Genestack documentation and domain inference

provenance.primary_data_source: docs

known_data_gaps.gap_description: K8s Secrets storing TLS private keys may not be encrypted at rest in etcd; if etcd encryption is not enabled, all Genestack TLS private keys are stored in cleartext
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Verify etcd encryption-at-rest is enabled for K8s Secrets; audit EncryptionConfiguration for Secret resources

known_data_gaps.gap_description: cert-manager version and patch level unknown; outdated versions may have known CVEs affecting certificate issuance security
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Confirm cert-manager Helm chart version from Genestack deployment; verify against latest stable release for security patches

entities.name: OVN Virtual Networking (OpenStack)

Deployed via Kube-OVN CNI plugin (migrated from Kubespray to Helm chart management). OVS/OVN switch layer shown in Genestack architecture diagram. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes (Kube-OVN Helm migration), docs.rackspacecloud.com/genestack-architecture.

authentication_mechanisms.protocol: OVN SSL for northbound/southbound DB access

encryption_profile.data_in_transit: Geneve tunnel encapsulation for overlay traffic; OVN control plane communication; IPsec available for tunnel encryption
encryption_profile.key_management: OVN certificate-based authentication for control plane; IPsec key management if enabled

availability_design.redundancy_level: OVN northbound/southbound DB cluster with Raft consensus; OVS on every compute/network node; Kube-OVN CNI on all K8s nodes

observability_stack.logging_coverage: OVN northbound/southbound logs, OVS vswitchd logs, Kube-OVN controller logs

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: OVN/OVS failure causes TOTAL NETWORK OUTAGE for all OpenStack and Kubernetes workloads in Genestack. All VM networking, tenant network isolation, floating IPs, security groups, and K8s pod networking depend on OVN. This is the single most critical network component.

entities.name: MariaDB Galera Cluster (OpenStack)
entities.description: Synchronous multi-master database cluster for all OpenStack services in Genestack. Managed by MariaDB Operator. Provides HA database backend for Keystone, Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Glance, Heat, and all other OpenStack service databases. rackspacecloud.com/infrastructure-mariadb, docs.rackspacecloud.com/genestack-structure-and-files.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: MariaDB data-at-rest encryption (InnoDB tablespace encryption) — available but enablement status requires confirmation
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for Galera replication (SST/IST); TLS for client connections from OpenStack services
encryption_profile.key_management: MariaDB file-based key management or encryption plugin; master key rotation support

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multi-master Galera cluster (minimum 3 nodes) with synchronous replication; quorum-based split-brain protection

observability_stack.logging_coverage: MariaDB error log, slow query log, Galera replication state changes
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: Galera cluster size, replication lag, wsrep flow control, query throughput, connection pool utilization
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Galera node departure, split-brain detection, replication lag thresholds, disk space alerts

support_model.escalation_path: Genestack L1 → DBA/MariaDB SME → MariaDB community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: MariaDB Galera cluster failure is a TOTAL OUTAGE of ALL OpenStack services. Every OpenStack service (Keystone, Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Glance, Heat, Barbican, Octavia, etc.) depends on MariaDB for state persistence. Complete loss of database quorum means zero cloud operations possible.

entities.access_control_model: MySQL GRANT-based privilege system; per-service database user with schema-level isolation; root access restricted to MariaDB Operator

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from central database role — MariaDB Galera stores ALL OpenStack service state including Keystone credentials, Barbican master KEK, and every service database

temporal.change_reason: Attribute enrichment — foundational OpenStack database, Galera HA architecture, and critical security attributes populated

provenance.primary_data_source: docs

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Whether MariaDB data-at-rest encryption is actually enabled not confirmed — all OpenStack service databases including Keystone credentials and Barbican secrets are stored here
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Verify InnoDB tablespace encryption status; confirm encryption key management plugin configuration; audit encrypted tables vs unencrypted

known_data_gaps.gap_description: MariaDB backup procedure, tested restore time, and point-in-time recovery capability not documented — this is the single most critical backup in the entire Genestack environment
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Document full backup/restore procedure; test Galera SST-based recovery; measure RTO; validate backup integrity with periodic restore tests

known_data_gaps.gap_description: MariaDB server version and MariaDB Operator version not documented
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Retrieve MariaDB version from cluster; verify compatibility with Galera and OpenStack requirements

entities.name: Memcached Cluster (OpenStack)
entities.description: Distributed memory caching system deployed as part of Genestack infrastructure for OpenStack service token/session caching. Deployed on openstack-control-plane labeled nodes. Monitored via prometheus-memcached-exporter. Install script: /opt/genestack/bin/install-memcached.sh. Used by Keystone, Nova, and other OpenStack services for session and query caching. rackspacecloud.com/infrastructure-memcached, docs.rackspacecloud.com/monitoring-info.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: N/A - in-memory cache only, no disk persistence
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: Memcached protocol does not support TLS natively; traffic unencrypted within K8s cluster network unless sidecar/service mesh provides encryption
encryption_profile.key_management: N/A

availability_design.redundancy_level: Multi-node cluster on openstack-control-plane labeled nodes

support_model.escalation_path: SRE → Platform Engineering → Community

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: Severe performance degradation across all OpenStack services; Keystone token caching lost (authentication latency spike); Nova and other services fall back to database for every request; potential cascading failure as MariaDB becomes overloaded

entities.access_control_model: No native authentication - access controlled via Kubernetes network policy restricting connectivity to OpenStack service pods only

backup_status.retention_period: N/A - volatile in-memory cache, repopulates on restart

temporal.change_reason: KG enrichment - attribute population from Genestack documentation and domain inference

provenance.primary_data_source: docs

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Memcached protocol transmits Keystone tokens and session data in cleartext within K8s network; no native TLS support means sensitive authentication tokens traverse network unencrypted
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Evaluate memcached TLS proxy sidecar (stunnel/Envoy sidecar) or migrate to Redis with TLS for sensitive token caching; alternatively verify K8s network policy provides sufficient isolation

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Memcached has no authentication mechanism; any pod with network access can read/write/flush cache including Keystone tokens
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Implement strict Kubernetes NetworkPolicy to restrict Memcached access to authorized OpenStack service pods only; audit existing network policies

entities.name: OpenStack Skyline Dashboard
entities.description: Next-generation web dashboard for OpenStack with modern responsive UI, faster load times, and enhanced responsiveness. Extends beyond Horizon (sys-132) with Skyline-specific features. Rackspace-maintained fork at github.com/skyline-rxt. SAML Federation support via HTTP Routes with cookie-driven session persistence. rackspacecloud.com/release-notes (SAML/Shibboleth integration), github.com/skyline-rxt.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: Minimal persistent state — session data via cookies; backend in MariaDB
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for HTTPS; SAML assertions over TLS; cookie-driven session persistence via HTTP Routes
encryption_profile.key_management: Session key management; SAML/Shibboleth federation certificate management

entities.name: VMware vSAN Software-Defined Storage
entities.description: Enterprise-grade hyper-converged software-defined storage natively embedded in vSphere hypervisor for RPC-VMware environments. Part of VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC architecture alongside vCenter and NSX. Provides distributed storage for VM workloads. rackspace.com SDDC product documentation.

encryption_profile.data_at_rest: vSAN data-at-rest encryption (AES-256) — encrypts all data on disk groups including dedup/compression cache
encryption_profile.data_in_transit: vSAN data-in-transit encryption for inter-host replication traffic
encryption_profile.key_management: vSphere Native Key Provider or external KMIP-compliant KMS (e.g., HyTrust, Thales)

availability_design.redundancy_level: Distributed RAID (RAID-1/RAID-5/RAID-6 erasure coding) across fault domains; FTT=1 minimum

observability_stack.logging_tool: vRealize Log Insight / vSAN health service logs
observability_stack.logging_coverage: vSAN disk group health, resync operations, dedup/compression stats
observability_stack.monitoring_coverage: IOPS, latency, throughput per disk group; capacity utilization; resync progress
observability_stack.alerting_coverage: Disk failures, degraded objects, capacity thresholds, network partitions

support_model.escalation_path: Rackspace NOC → VMware L2 → Broadcom engineering

business_impact_if_unavailable.impact_description: vSAN failure would render all VM persistent storage inaccessible across the affected Workload Domain. All VMs would lose disk I/O, causing complete workload outage. Data loss risk if below FTT redundancy threshold.

entities.access_control_model: RBAC — vSAN management integrated with vCenter permissions; storage policy-based management (SPBM) controls VM placement

cyber_exposure.assessment_methodology: Inferred from storage infrastructure role — vSAN underpins all VM persistent storage in SDDC
